incidents.victim: U.S. and Ukrainian Oil & Gas Critical Infrastructure
incidents.vector: Targeted attacks on oil and gas industrial control systems and physical infrastructure across the US, Ukraine, and other countries.
incidents.impact: Caused physical and operational damage to critical energy infrastructure in multiple countries. Russian national Artem Revenskii ('Digit') pleaded guilty on May 1, 2026 and faces up to 27 years in prison.

incidents.victim: Web Hosting Organizations Worldwide (cPanel/WHM users)
incidents.vector: Authentication bypass via injected newline characters in the cPanel login form password field, allowing unauthenticated session promotion without credential verification; affects all cPanel/WHM versions since 11.40 (2013), CVSS 9.8.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed before patch availability; approximately 1.5 million cPanel instances exposed online; CISA added CVE-2026-41940 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog; hosting providers Namecheap and KnownHost confirmed in-the-wild compromises.

incidents.victim: U.S. Medical, Pharmaceutical, and Engineering Companies
incidents.vector: Two cybersecurity professionals — an incident response manager (Sygnia) and a ransomware negotiator (DigitalMint) — deployed BlackCat ransomware against victim organizations and betrayed clients by sharing confidential negotiation details and insurance policy limits with ransomware operators.
incidents.impact: Multiple US healthcare and engineering firms extorted; patient data from a California doctor’s office publicly leaked; $1.2 million ransom paid by one victim; Goldberg and Martin each sentenced to 4 years on April 30, 2026; co-conspirator Martino enabled $75.3 million in total ransom payments and faces sentencing July 9.

incidents.victim: Multiple U.S. Organizations — retail, hospitality, aviation, financial services, and technology sectors
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (The Com affiliates)
incidents.vector: Voice-phishing and social engineering directing employees to adversary-in-the-middle phishing pages mimicking corporate SSO portals; attackers remove and re-register MFA devices, traverse SaaS ecosystems, and exfiltrate data; some victims subjected to follow-on DDoS attacks and employee swatting.
incidents.impact: Ongoing data theft and extortion across multiple US critical infrastructure sectors since October 2025; extortion demands typically in the seven-figure range; campaign is affiliated with the broader Scattered Spider ecosystem and linked to ShinyHunters.

incidents.victim: Delta Dental Insurance Company / Delta Dental of New York
incidents.vector: Exploitation of a MOVEit Transfer zero-day vulnerability in mid-2023 to exfiltrate files from servers used to transfer data among affiliates, business partners, and healthcare providers.
incidents.impact: Consumer PII — including Social Security numbers, driver’s license numbers, financial account details, and patient health data — exfiltrated from both Delta Dental entities. NYDFS imposed a $2.25 million penalty on April 30, 2026 for inadequate incident response programs and failure to timely report the breach.

incidents.victim: Russian Government Agencies and Aviation Companies
incidents.vector: Phishing emails with infected archive files, malicious advertising campaigns mimicking aviation software, and trojanized tools hosted on SourceForge; malware collects GIS/geospatial data, keystrokes, browser credentials, and screenshots.
incidents.impact: Sensitive geographic information system data stolen from Russian government and aviation targets since September 2025; malware distribution lures also suggest targeting of military drone operators and satellite communications personnel.

incidents.victim: SAP Developer Ecosystem (≥570K weekly npm downloads affected)
incidents.vector: Four widely-used SAP CAP/MBT npm packages (@cap-js/sqlite, @cap-js/postgres, @cap-js/db-service, mbt) compromised with malicious preinstall scripts; multi-stage payload exfiltrates GitHub tokens, npm credentials, cloud access keys, and CI/CD pipeline secrets to attacker-controlled repositories; Russian locale killswitch included.
incidents.impact: Over 570,000 weekly downloads affected; developer credentials and cloud secrets exposed across potentially tens of thousands of projects globally. Campaign is entering active monetization phase as of May 2, 2026; CISA added to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

incidents.victim: ADT Inc.
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — social engineering and data extortion
incidents.vector: Vishing attack targeting ADT employees with adversary-in-the-middle phishing pages mimicking Okta single sign-on; attackers removed and re-registered MFA devices to maintain persistent access to corporate systems.
incidents.impact: Data on approximately 5.5 million customers exposed.

incidents.victim: COVID-19 Research Institutions, Law Firms; global Exchange Server users (Hafnium campaign)
incidents.attacker: China — Ministry of State Security (Shanghai State Security Bureau)
incidents.vector: Targeted intrusions into COVID-19 research institutions and law firms under MSS direction; also participated in the Hafnium Exchange Server exploitation campaign targeting thousands of organizations worldwide.
incidents.impact: Sensitive pandemic research and confidential legal communications compromised; Chinese national Xu Zewei was extradited from Italy to the US on April 29, 2026 to face prosecution for MSS-directed espionage.

incidents.victim: U.S. Federal Civilian Executive Branch Agencies (Cisco Firepower/ASA devices)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of Cisco Firepower FXOS zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362) to deploy the FIRESTARTER custom backdoor; attackers maintained persistence even after patches were applied.
incidents.impact: Custom backdoor installed on FCEB agency Cisco network devices with post-patching persistence; CISA issued Emergency Directive 25-03 update; joint advisory co-sealed with NCSC-UK warning all organizations running Cisco Firepower/ASA products.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp, and others (2022 campaign)
incidents.vector: SIM swapping, vishing, and social engineering targeting major US technology companies in 2022 to steal employee credentials, hijack accounts, and steal cryptocurrency from high-value targets.
incidents.impact: Multiple major technology companies breached in 2022; over $8 million in cryptocurrency stolen; 24-year-old British national Tyler Buchanan pleaded guilty in April 2026 to wire fraud and identity theft charges.

incidents.victim: Moldova National Health Insurance Company (CNAM)
incidents.vector: Unknown intrusion method; data exfiltrated from CNAM’s compulsory health insurance database; no ransom demand issued, suggesting intelligence-gathering motivation.
incidents.impact: Approximately 30% of Moldova’s national health insurance database stolen — containing personal information and payment records for a significant share of the country’s 2.4 million citizens. Assessed as Moldova’s most serious cyber incident this year; officials note cyberattacks on the country’s critical infrastructure have increased sharply since 2022.

incidents.victim: German Government — Bundestag President Julia Klöckner
incidents.vector: Compromise of Signal encrypted messaging account used to gain access to a sensitive group chat including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and other senior government officials.
incidents.impact: Russia gained unauthorized access to encrypted internal communications among Germany’s most senior government officials; full scope of intelligence collected unknown.

incidents.victim: Israeli Water and Desalination Facilities
incidents.vector: ZionSiphon OT malware deployed against industrial control systems at Israeli water treatment and desalination facilities; attack failed due to a critical bug in the malware that caused it to self-delete before executing its destructive payload.
incidents.impact: No physical damage or disruption achieved; malware self-deleted before executing. The attempted attack demonstrates continued Iranian targeting of Israeli critical water infrastructure OT systems.

incidents.victim: France — Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (national identity document agency)
incidents.vector: Unknown intrusion into France’s national identity and driver’s license management system.
incidents.impact: Attackers exfiltrating and selling French citizens’ identity documents and driver’s license data on cybercrime forums; full scope of breach under investigation.

incidents.victim: Sri Lanka Ministry of Finance
incidents.vector: Hackers compromised Sri Lanka’s Finance Ministry systems and diverted a scheduled foreign debt repayment, redirecting $2.5 million to accounts in Australia.
incidents.impact: $2.5 million in government funds stolen with diplomatic and financial implications for Sri Lanka’s debt management obligations.

incidents.victim: Carnival Corporation; also Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy, and Medtronic (same campaign)
incidents.vector: Infostealer malware compromise of employee credentials enabling unauthorized access to corporate systems; part of a coordinated ShinyHunters campaign targeting multiple industries.
incidents.impact: 7.5 million Carnival customers’ data stolen; same campaign also breached Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy, and Medtronic; data being sold or leveraged for extortion.

incidents.victim: Vercel Inc. and downstream customers
incidents.vector: Infostealer malware infected a Context.ai employee’s device, exposing credentials that provided access to Vercel’s cloud platform infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Cloud infrastructure breach with confirmed fallout expanding to Vercel customers and third-party connected systems; full scope of customer data exposure still being determined as of May 2, 2026.

incidents.victim: U.S. Medicare (CMS) — Healthcare Provider Database
incidents.vector: Database misconfiguration or breach exposed the Medicare provider portal containing healthcare provider Social Security numbers.
incidents.impact: Healthcare provider Social Security numbers exposed via the U.S. Medicare provider portal; scope and responsible party under investigation.

incidents.victim: Hasbro Inc.
incidents.vector: Ransomware attack on Hasbro corporate systems.
incidents.impact: Systems still being restored as of May 2026; Q1 2026 financial results delayed due to ongoing recovery; significant business disruption to the toy manufacturer.

incidents.victim: Huge Networks (Brazilian DDoS protection firm) and Brazilian ISPs
incidents.vector: Hackers compromised Huge Networks’ own infrastructure using a Mirai botnet variant exploiting CVE-2023-1389, then repurposed the hijacked DDoS protection infrastructure to attack Brazilian ISPs.
incidents.impact: Major Brazilian ISPs disrupted; a DDoS protection firm’s own infrastructure turned against the internet providers it served.
